RIOT.title: Riot Platforms Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript

macro_events.event_summary: Gold posted its biggest two-month drop on record, though some analysts still see long-term upside potential of a doubling over five years.
primary_drivers: Sharp decline in spot gold price compresses miner margins and AISC leverage
Reduced safe-haven demand suggests risk-on rotation / lower tail-risk pricing
Potential unwind of crowded long-gold positioning

beneficiaries.rationale: Large money-center bank benefits from risk-on rotation away from safe havens; gold weakness often coincides with stronger USD and risk appetite that supports trading/IB revenue.

losers.rationale: Newmont is the largest pure-play gold miner; earnings have direct operating leverage to gold price (every $100/oz drop compresses margins materially given ~$1,400 AISC). Biggest 2-month drop ever directly hits Q2 realized prices.
Barrick Gold is a near-pure-play producer with similar AISC leverage; revenue and EPS estimates revise down mechanically with spot price decline of this magnitude.
Agnico Eagle is a pure-play senior gold producer; same direct price exposure on realized ounces sold, and high-cost Canadian operations amplify margin compression.
Wheaton Precious Metals is a streaming company whose revenue per ounce delivered tracks spot gold; record decline directly compresses cash margin per ounce on its silver/gold streams.
Franco-Nevada royalty/streaming model has revenue tied to spot gold prices; though lower operating leverage than miners, NAV and dividend coverage compress with sharp price drops.

headlines: Gold posts its biggest 2-month drop ever. How its price could still double over the next 5 years.
